evals.prompt: Create a module called `calculator` that provides a Calculator service class with add() and subtract() methods accepting two numbers. Register the service in the module's services.yml file. Then write a kernel test that verifies the calculator service can be loaded from the Drupal service container and that its methods return correct results. Use the correct test base class for testing a service without browser interaction. Put the test in the proper directory following Drupal test conventions. We're running Drupal 10 and want D11 compatibility.
evals.expected_output: A calculator module with a service class, services.yml, and a kernel test extending KernelTestBase
expectations: Test class extends KernelTestBase (NOT BrowserTestBase or WebDriverTestBase -- kernel tests are the LOWEST sufficient level for service testing, BrowserTestBase installs full Drupal + browser which is 10-100x slower and unnecessary for testing a service)

Test class has protected static $modules array including 'calculator' (NOT installing modules in setUp() via module_installer service -- the $modules property is the correct KernelTestBase pattern for declaring required modules)

setUp() calls parent::setUp() as the FIRST line (required for KernelTestBase to initialize the service container and database -- calling it after other setup or forgetting it causes cryptic 'service not found' errors)

Test loads the calculator service from the container using $this->container->get() with the registered service ID (NOT instantiating the class directly with new Calculator() which bypasses DI and doesn't test service registration)
Test class has @group annotation in the class-level PHPDoc (required for test discovery -- without @group, the test is invisible to both phpunit --group and Drupal's test runner)

Test file is in tests/src/Kernel/ directory with namespace Drupal\Tests\calculator\Kernel (NOT in tests/src/Unit/ which would be wrong test type for service container testing, NOT in tests/src/Functional/ which is over-testing)
The module enables successfully: ddev drush en calculator returns exit code 0
cli_checks: Run the kernel test via ddev exec phpunit (or ddev drush test:run) — verify the test discovers, executes, and passes with green results (this IS the E2E verification for a testing skill — the test itself must actually work)
